content:
Grateful to share that the Hon’ble High Court has granted interim protection by staying the operation and effect of a revisional order that permitted the prosecution to obtain the mandatory sanction under Section 217 BNSS after cognizance had already been taken for an offence under Section 299 BNS.

The challenge was straightforward: where Parliament has made previous sanction a condition precedent for taking cognizance, can a Revisional Court create a three-month window for the prosecution to comply with a statutory mandate that had already been breached?

The Hon’ble High Court has stayed the impugned directions and all consequential proceedings.
